articles.title: Celtics Tie NBA Record with 29 Threes, Clinch No. 2 Seed
articles.original_title: Celtics tie NBA record with 29 3s; clinch 2-seed
articles.summary: Boston matched the league record by sinking 29 three‑pointers in a dominant victory over New Orleans. The win secured the Eastern Conference’s No. 2 playoff seed for the Celtics.
key_points: Boston made 29 threes, tying both the franchise and NBA record.
The blowout win over the Pelicans clinched the East’s second seed.
The performance highlighted the Celtics’ perimeter shooting strength heading into the playoffs.

articles.title: Redick: Opponents Prefer Facing Injured Lakers in First Round
articles.original_title: Redick: 'Everybody' wants to face hobbled Lakers
articles.summary: Lakers head coach JJ Redick said many Western Conference teams would rather meet Los Angeles in the opening round, even with the club’s injury issues. He suggested the Lakers are viewed as a favorable first‑round matchup as seeding is still being decided.
key_points: Redick believes other West teams see the Lakers as an attractive first‑round opponent.
The comment reflects the uncertainty around the Lakers’ health and seeding.
No specific opponents or dates were mentioned.

articles.title: Joel Embiid Discharged from Hospital, Return Timeline Unclear
articles.original_title: 76ers' Embiid out of hospital; no timeline to play
articles.summary: Philadelphia 76ers star Joel Embiid left a Houston hospital and is traveling back to Philadelphia. The team indicated there is no set timetable for his return to action.
key_points: Embiid was released from the hospital and is back in Philadelphia.
The Sixers have not provided a projected date for his comeback.
His health status remains the primary focus for the organization.

articles.title: Bulls Coach Billy Donovan Acknowledges Reinsdorf’s Endorsement
articles.original_title: Donovan not reading into Reinsdorf's endorsement
articles.summary: Chicago Bulls head coach Billy Donovan, whose future with the team is still undecided, thanked team president Michael Reinsdorf for his public support. Donovan emphasized that all parties must consider what’s best for the franchise.
key_points: Donovan received an endorsement from Bulls president Michael Reinsdorf.
He has not yet decided whether to remain with Chicago.
Donovan stressed the importance of acting in the Bulls’ best interest.

articles.title: SGA, Jokic Among 168 NBA Players Out Friday
articles.original_title: SGA, Jokic among at least 168 out NBA-wide Fri.
articles.summary: A minimum of 168 NBA players missed Friday's games, including reigning MVP Shai Gilgeous‑Alexander, three‑time MVP Nikola Jokic, and 14 Memphis Grizzlies. The widespread absences reflect ongoing injury and rest protocols across the league.
key_points: At least 168 players were held out of action on Friday.
Shai Gilgeous‑Alexander and Nikola Jokic were among the high‑profile absences.
Fourteen members of the Memphis Grizzlies also missed the games.

articles.title: Wembanyama Reaches 65‑Game Eligibility Mark with 40‑Point Performance
articles.original_title: Wembanyama checks another 'box,' hits 65 games
articles.summary: Victor Wembanyama hit the 65‑game threshold for award eligibility after scoring 40 points and grabbing 13 rebounds in a 139‑120 win over the Dallas Mavericks. The milestone secures his eligibility for season‑end honors.
key_points: Wembanyama recorded 40 points and 13 rebounds against Dallas.
The performance gave him his 65th game of the season, meeting award eligibility requirements.
The Spurs defeated the Mavericks 139‑120.

articles.title: Heat Waive Guard Terry Rozier Amid Gambling Probe
articles.original_title: Heat waive Rozier, open roster spot for addition
articles.summary: The Miami Heat have waived guard Terry Rozier, who has been on leave since October due to a sports‑gambling investigation. The move opens a roster spot for a new addition.
key_points: Terry Rozier was waived after being on leave since October.
The waiver is linked to an ongoing sports‑gambling probe.
Miami now has an open roster spot for a replacement player.

articles.title: NBA Fines Orlando Magic $25K for Injury Report Violation
articles.original_title: NBA fines Magic $25K for injury report violation
articles.summary: The NBA fined the Orlando Magic $25,000 for breaching league injury‑reporting rules prior to their Monday night home game against the Detroit Pistons. The sanction underscores the league’s enforcement of transparency standards.
key_points: Orlando Magic were penalized $25,000 for an injury‑report violation.
The infraction occurred before the Monday home game versus Detroit.
The fine reflects the NBA’s commitment to accurate injury disclosures.

articles.title: LeBron Leads Lakers Past Suns 101‑73, Securing Home‑Court Advantage
articles.original_title: LeBron James scores 28, leads Lakers past Suns 101-73 to clinch home-court advantage in 1st round
articles.summary: LeBron James posted 28 points, 12 assists and six rebounds as the short‑handed Los Angeles Lakers defeated the Phoenix Suns 101‑73, clinching home‑court advantage for the first round of the playoffs. Luke Kennard added 19 points and Rui Hachimura contributed 13.
key_points: Lakers beat Suns 101‑73, earning home‑court advantage in the first round.
LeBron James recorded 28 points, 12 assists and six rebounds.
Luke Kennard scored 19 points; Rui Hachimura added 13.

Result gives the Lakers home‑court advantage in the opening playoff series.

articles.title: LeBron James Becomes 4th Player in NBA History with 12,000 Assists
articles.original_title: LeBron James becomes 4th NBA player to reach 12,000 career assists - Reuters
articles.summary: LeBron James reached the 12,000‑assist milestone, joining only three other players in NBA history to do so. The achievement was reported by Reuters and adds another historic statistic to his résumé.
key_points: LeBron recorded his 12,000th career assist, placing him fourth all‑time.
He joins John Stockton, Jason Kidd and Mark Jackson on the exclusive list.
The milestone emphasizes his longevity and playmaking impact over his career.

articles.title: Lakers May Take Drastic Measures to Preserve NBA Season, but at High Cost
articles.original_title: Lakers might go this far to save NBA season, but cost might be great - New York Post
articles.summary: The New York Post discussed potential extreme actions the Los Angeles Lakers could consider to keep the NBA season on track, noting that such steps could have significant financial and roster implications. The article explores the balance between competitive goals and organizational risk.
key_points: Speculation about the Lakers making major moves to influence the season’s continuation.
Potential costs include salary‑cap strain, roster depth loss, and long‑term financial impact.
The piece highlights the tension between short‑term competitive desires and long‑term franchise health.
